title: Marketing-sourced deals close 14 days faster this week
anchor: Nine marketing-sourced wins this week averaged 38 days to close, with seven coming from mid-market accounts.
connect: The prior 11 weeks averaged 51.9 days across 63 deals.
body: Nine marketing-sourced wins this week averaged 38 days to close, with seven coming from mid-market accounts. The prior 11 weeks averaged 51.9 days across 63 deals.
trace: Current week marketing-sourced wins show significant velocity improvement versus trailing 11-week baseline.

title: Mid-market SQL volume up 50% in April
anchor: April generated 147 mid-market SQLs through the 24th, running ahead of the quarterly average.
connect: January through March averaged 98 SQLs per month.
body: April generated 147 mid-market SQLs through the 24th, running ahead of the quarterly average. January through March averaged 98 SQLs per month.
trace: April mid-market SQL generation shows substantial acceleration versus Q1 baseline performance.

title: ABM leads accept at 61%, outperforming other channels
anchor: SDRs accepted 61.4% of the 70 ABM MQLs this month versus 41.6% on the remaining 77.
connect: The 20-point acceptance gap sits at the widest margin this quarter.
body: SDRs accepted 61.4% of the 70 ABM MQLs this month versus 41.6% on the remaining 77. The 20-point acceptance gap sits at the widest margin this quarter.
trace: ABM channel shows meaningfully higher SDR acceptance rate, creating channel quality differential.

title: Paid social pipeline creation doubles quarter over quarter
anchor: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline against $410K in Q1.
connect: Paid search moved the opposite direction, from $720K in Q1 to $380K in Q2.
body: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline against $410K in Q1. Paid search moved the opposite direction, from $720K in Q1 to $380K in Q2.
trace: Paid social channel acceleration coincides with paid search deceleration, showing channel mix shift.

title: Digital ad spend runs $84K under budget through April
anchor: Q2 digital ads spent $256K through April 23rd versus the $340K planned allocation.
connect: The $84K gap moved to events, specifically SaaS Connect and Signal Summit.
body: Q2 digital ads spent $256K through April 23rd versus the $340K planned allocation. The $84K gap moved to events, specifically SaaS Connect and Signal Summit.
trace: Under-spend in digital ads channeled to event programs, showing budget reallocation mid-quarter.

title: Branded search climbs for sixth consecutive week
anchor: Weekly branded search volume hit 1,921 in the latest period, up 92% over the six-week window.
connect: Growth accelerated each week, with no flat or declining periods in the sequence.
body: Weekly branded search volume hit 1,921 in the latest period, up 92% over the six-week window. Growth accelerated each week, with no flat or declining periods in the sequence.
trace: Branded search shows consistent weekly acceleration without interruption, indicating sustained brand momentum.

title: Atlas brand mentions lead competitive set by 52%
anchor: Atlas collected 1,840 brand mentions in April versus Beacon Systems at 1,210.
connect: The gap widened from March when both companies ran closer to parity.
body: Atlas collected 1,840 brand mentions in April versus Beacon Systems at 1,210. The gap widened from March when both companies ran closer to parity.
trace: Brand mention volume shows Atlas pulling ahead of primary competitor Beacon Systems in April.

title: Analyst mentions double over the past two weeks
anchor: Nine analyst mentions appeared in the last 14 days, with Forrester and G2 driving six of them.
connect: The prior 30 days generated six mentions total across all analyst firms.
body: Nine analyst mentions appeared in the last 14 days, with Forrester and G2 driving six of them. The prior 30 days generated six mentions total across all analyst firms.
trace: Recent analyst mention activity shows acceleration, particularly from Forrester and G2 coverage.

title: Direct traffic crosses organic search for the first time
anchor: Direct traffic accounts for 42.3% of April visits versus organic search at 36.5%.
connect: The crossover happened April 15th and has held for nine days running.
body: Direct traffic accounts for 42.3% of April visits versus organic search at 36.5%. The crossover happened April 15th and has held for nine days running.
trace: Direct traffic overtook organic search mid-April, marking first sustained reversal of traffic mix.

title: Event-sourced deals close 25 days faster than Q4
anchor: Q1 event-sourced wins averaged 46.4 days to close across 17 deals.
connect: Q4 event deals took 71.7 days across 15 wins.
body: Q1 event-sourced wins averaged 46.4 days to close across 17 deals. Q4 event deals took 71.7 days across 15 wins.
trace: Event-sourced deal velocity improved substantially from Q4 to Q1, showing channel efficiency gains.

title: SaaS Connect drove 70% of Q1 event wins
anchor: Twelve of the 17 Q1 event wins traced back to SaaS Connect attendance.
connect: Peer events contributed the remaining five deals across the quarter.
body: Twelve of the 17 Q1 event wins traced back to SaaS Connect attendance. Peer events contributed the remaining five deals across the quarter.
trace: SaaS Connect dominates event channel performance, generating most Q1 event-attributed wins.

title: Atlas Insights leads reference opt-in rates at 75%
anchor: Three-quarters of Atlas Insights customers agree to serve as references.
connect: Atlas Connect lags at 28.9% while Workflow sits at 70%.
body: Three-quarters of Atlas Insights customers agree to serve as references. Atlas Connect lags at 28.9% while Workflow sits at 70%.
trace: Product-level reference willingness varies significantly, with Insights customers most willing to advocate.

title: Named account engagement hits threshold on two prospects
anchor: Two of the three target accounts with high-intent activity sit on the Named Accounts list.
connect: The Named Accounts universe includes 50 total prospects across segments.
body: Two of the three target accounts with high-intent activity sit on the Named Accounts list. The Named Accounts universe includes 50 total prospects across segments.
trace: High-intent engagement concentrates within the Named Accounts list, showing targeting effectiveness.

title: Atlas Assist launch campaign ready two weeks out
anchor: May 8th launch date approaches with campaign assets complete and calendar locked.
connect: Calendar locks happen this Thursday, April 24th.
body: May 8th launch date approaches with campaign assets complete and calendar locked. Calendar locks happen this Thursday, April 24th.
trace: Upcoming product launch shows campaign preparation on schedule with calendar finalization imminent.

title: SDR capacity exceeds inbound volume by 48%
anchor: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 leads versus 142 inbound volume.
connect: The four-week average shows capacity at 206 and volume at 201, running much tighter.
body: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 leads versus 142 inbound volume. The four-week average shows capacity at 206 and volume at 201, running much tighter.
trace: SDR capacity currently exceeds inbound lead flow, contrasting with typical near-capacity utilization.

title: Mid-market fintech accounts dominate recent wins
anchor: Eight of the 12 mid-market closed-wins in the last 60 days come from fintech.
connect: All 12 accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
body: Eight of the 12 mid-market closed-wins in the last 60 days come from fintech. All 12 accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
trace: Recent mid-market wins show strong fintech vertical concentration with consistent tech stack profile.

title: Enterprise win rate climbs to 31% in Q2
anchor: Eight of 26 enterprise deals closed-won this quarter, improving from the trailing four-quarter rate.
connect: The four-quarter enterprise win rate averaged 22%.
body: Eight of 26 enterprise deals closed-won this quarter, improving from the trailing four-quarter rate. The four-quarter enterprise win rate averaged 22%.
trace: Enterprise segment win rate shows improvement in Q2 versus historical four-quarter performance.

title: Enterprise deal value averages $187K in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise wins average $187K per deal across eight closed opportunities.
connect: The trailing four quarters averaged $142K per enterprise win.
body: Q2 enterprise wins average $187K per deal across eight closed opportunities. The trailing four quarters averaged $142K per enterprise win.
trace: Enterprise deal values increase substantially in Q2 versus historical average deal sizes.
